summary: End-to-end testing of Twin Cities Insurance app (backend + frontend). All 12 backend pytest tests pass. Frontend Landing CTA opens 3-step quote modal, full submit flow succeeds (POST /api/quotes returns 201, success screen shows 'Application Received Securely'). 6 portfolio cards present and each opens the modal. Footer shows phone 612-222-1749, email mumerahmadzai@gmail.com, agent Muhammad Umar Ahmadzai. Admin /admin page loads and lists submitted quotes (including the newly-submitted Playwright Test LLC); Refresh button works.

issues: Three.js console warnings: THREE.Clock deprecated -> use THREE.Timer; THREE.WebGLShadowMap PCFSoftShadowMap deprecated -> falls back to PCFShadowMap. Cosmetic only, no functional impact.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py stores created_at as ISO string in Mongo then re-parses to datetime in GET responses; acceptable but mixing types in the DB can cause subtle ordering issues if some docs were inserted as native datetime. Consider standardizing on native datetime.
Admin page table uses div rows (not <tr>) — fine, but adding data-testid='quote-row-{id}' to each row would make automated assertions cleaner.
CORS allow_origins falls back to '*' when CORS_ORIGINS env unset — acceptable for landing page but worth tightening for production.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests created multiple TEST_-prefixed quotes via POST /api/quotes; one 'Playwright Test LLC' quote via UI submission. All visible in /admin.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Quote modal animation requires ~1s wait after CTA click for AnimatePresence to mount. Admin route uses div-based rows; query with [data-testid^='quote-row'] would need to be added. WebGL warnings on console are benign three.js deprecations. Backend GET /api/quotes returns created_at as datetime ISO string after server-side parsing — date sort assertion in tests uses lexical comparison of ISO strings (works correctly).
